Prie le Haut-Commissariat des Nations Unies aux droits de l'homme d'établir un  rapport  sur  l'application  des  recommandations  figurant  dans  le  rapport  du  Comité consultatif du Conseil des droits de l'homme 3 , qui sera publié dans une version facile à lire et  sous  une  forme  accessible,  en  étroite  coopération  avec  le  Groupe  de  travail  sur  la discrimination  à  l'égard  des  femmes  et  des  filles,  le  Comité  pour  l'élimination  de  la discrimination  à  l'égard des  femmes  et  le  Comité  consultatif,  et  en  consultation  avec  les principales parties prenantes, et de le lui présenter à sa soixante-deuxième session ;

17. Décide de poursuivre l'examen de la question de l'élimination de toutes les formes  de  discrimination  à  l'égard  des  femmes  et  des  filles  à  titre  hautement  prioritaire, conformément à son programme de travail, à sa soixante-deuxième session.
items_en: (b) Ensure access to justice and accountability mechanisms, as well as timely and effective remedies for the effective implementation and enforcement of laws to prevent and eliminate all forms of gender-based discrimination and violence, including by adopting a survivor-centred approach, providing women and girls with information in accessible form on their rights under relevant laws and improving the legislative framework, and integrating age, disability and gender-sensitive training into judicial systems to ensure equality before the law and equal protection of women and girls by law;

(c) To modify social and cultural attitudes that create, encourage or perpetuate racist, xenophobic and patriarchal stereotypes and those related to disability, age and gender and all other harmful social norms, attitudes or behaviours, or unequal power relationships that result in women and girls being considered subordinates or underlying and perpetuating multiple and cross-cutting forms of discrimination and violence against women and girls;

5. Recognizes that the reduction of socio-economic inequalities is an essential element of effective action to overcome poverty among women and girls, and reaffirms the importance of achieving gender equality and of respecting and promoting the full enjoyment of human rights by women and girls in poverty eradication measures, development policies and laws and measures to overcome inequalities in countries and from one country to another;

6. Reaffirms that it is essential to guarantee the right to social security, taking into account gender issues, to combat poverty by enabling women and girls throughout their lives to enjoy the human right to an adequate standard of living, including adequate food, clothing and housing, the rights to safe drinking water and sanitation, and the right to the enjoyment of the highest attainable standard of physical and mental health, inter alia, and that the right to an adequate standard of living includes access to and maintenance of benefits, in cash or in kind, without discrimination, in order to ensure protection from lack of labour-related income, the excessive cost of health care and insufficient family support;

7. Calls upon States to take immediate and gender-sensitive measures to eradicate poverty among women and girls, address gender inequalities and socio-economic inequalities and achieve substantive equality, including through:

(a) To identify and address, with the full and effective participation of women and girls, on an equal basis, the root causes of poverty and the particular challenges faced by women and girls living in poverty and suffering from aggravated inequalities;

(b) Seek to ensure access to universal, accessible, adequate, available and comprehensive social protection throughout the life of women and girls, regardless of their marital status, employment or participation in the formal labour market;

(c) To eliminate all forms of discrimination, in law or in practice, against women and girls, with regard to access to land rights, seeds, resources, housing and property, including at the time of marriage, during marriage and at the time of its dissolution;

(d) Develop and implement comprehensive, robust, resilient and gender-, disability- and age-sensitive care and support systems, with full respect for human rights, based on the principles of equality, non-discrimination and universality, and promoting shared responsibility and autonomy;

(e) Ensuring the availability, affordability, accessibility, acceptability and quality of health-related information, services and products, including those necessary to address "menstrual insecurity";

(f) To adopt all necessary measures to prevent, combat and eliminate sexual and gender-based violence, including domestic violence, against women and girls in poverty through adequately funded public services and social protection systems, including shelters and access to legal counsel and legal redress mechanisms;
(g) Repeal, amend or revise laws criminalizing acts related to poverty and survival and criminalizing or restricting the exercise of sexual and reproductive health and reproductive rights;

(h) Develop and implement appropriate gender-sensitive systems to measure poverty and equality in households and multidimensional poverty, and establish appropriate participatory monitoring and evaluation mechanisms to oversee the implementation of poverty reduction programmes and policies, assess their impact on women and girls and adopt remedial measures, including adequate funding and gender-sensitive budgeting;

(i) Promote the participation and consultation of women and girls in the design, development and implementation of laws and economic and social policies at the local and national levels, and promote gender equality in decision-making bodies;
(j) Promoting transparent, proportionate, progressive and gender-sensitive taxation regimes;

(k) Take regulatory measures to combat gender-based discrimination in the provision of services, including health services, by private sector actors, with a view to ensuring that the services they provide are accessible, adequate and regularly evaluated to meet the needs and human rights of women and girls, both in urban and rural areas;

(l) Prioritize poverty eradication and respect, protection and realization of human rights in the formulation and implementation of international assistance and cooperation programmes and agreements, paying particular attention to the situation of women and girls living in poverty;

(m) To conduct or support other research led by women on the effects of structural adjustment, taxation, debt, macroeconomic policies, trade agreements and investment agreements on human rights and gender equality, and to improve the situation of women and girls living in poverty;

8. Calls upon all States, in accordance with a human rights-based approach, to continue to develop standards and methods for the design and implementation of population censuses and household surveys, as well as the collection, analysis and dissemination of gender, age and disability-disaggregated statistics and data, and to improve existing standards and methods, by strengthening national statistical capacity, including by intensifying the mobilization of all sources for the provision of financial and technical assistance to developing countries, in order to enable them to design and collect, systematically and in a timely manner, reliable and high-quality data disaggregated by sex, age, disability, income and other relevant characteristics in the national context, and to ensure access to such data;

9. Urges businesses to contribute to tax justice by respecting their tax obligations, as well as to the realization of the human rights of all women and girls, including through participatory procedures for assessing the impact on women and men and human rights and due diligence procedures, in accordance with the Guiding Principles on Business
human rights, and ensuring that complaint mechanisms and remedies for damage caused by business activities are accessible, effective and gender-sensitive;
10. Calls upon the international and regional institutions specialized in the economic, financial and monetary fields, as well as their member States, to consider:
(a) To adopt human rights-based approaches that give priority to sustainable development and the eradication of poverty and gender-related socio-economic and socio-political inequalities;

(b) To design, prepare and implement gender-sensitive budgets for the realization of women's and girls' human rights and gender equality, using gender-sensitive budgeting tools, adopting measures such as grants, assistance, development cooperation and gender-sensitive loans and financing, and establishing budgetary accountability mechanisms;

(c) To promote meaningful participation and consultation of women and girls in the design, development and implementation of laws and economic policies at the local and national levels, and to promote gender equality in decision-making bodies;

11. Requests the Working Group on Discrimination against Women and Girls to continue to take into account age and disability issues within its mandate and to mainstream these issues throughout its work, and to consider the specific forms of discrimination faced by girls, and welcomes its decision to analyse the issue of discrimination against women and girls in the digital sphere, including new and emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence, and the impact of current and future developments on their rights, well-being and potential;

12. Calls upon all States to cooperate with and assist the Working Group in its work, to provide all available information requested by the Working Group and to give serious consideration to responding favourably to its requests for country visits in order to enable it to carry out its mandate effectively, invites relevant United Nations agencies, funds and programmes, in particular the United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN-Women), treaty bodies and special procedures, within their respective mandates, and civil society actors, including women's and girls' rights organizations, as well as indigenous peoples and the private sector, to cooperate fully with the Working Group in the fulfilment of its mandate, and requests the Working Group to continue to collaborate with the Commission on the Status of Women, including by participating in its work and submitting official reports to the Working Group;

13. Reiterates its request to the Secretary-General to ensure that the reports of the Working Group are brought to the attention of the Commission on the Status of Women and the General Assembly by means of a transmittal note, notes that, for the first time, the chairmanship of the Working Group addressed the Commission at its sixty-eighth session during the opening meeting, and requests the Working Group to continue to submit an oral report to the Commission and the General Assembly annually, at the same time as the reports of the Special Rapporteur on violence against women and girls, its causes and consequences and those of the Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination against Women;

14. Calls upon States to proactively seek, nominate and elect more candidates to fill vacancies in United Nations human rights bodies and mechanisms, including in senior management positions, in order to ensure equal representation of women;

15. Calls upon States to consider ways and means of enhancing the participation of women in its work, taking into account existing guidelines and standards as defined in its resolution 6/30 of 14 December 2007, in its decision 6/102 of 27 September 2007 and in the statement by President OS/12/1;

16. Requests the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights to prepare a report on the implementation of the recommendations contained in the report of the Advisory Committee to the Human Rights Council, 3 in an easy-to-read and accessible format, in close cooperation with the Working Group on Discrimination against Women and Girls, the Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination against Women and the Advisory Committee, and in consultation with key stakeholders, and to submit it to the General Assembly at its sixty-second session;

17. Decides to continue its consideration of the question of the elimination of all forms of discrimination against women and girls as a matter of high priority, in accordance with its programme of work, at its sixty-second session.
